name: Student Organization Resource Center
summary: The Student Organization Resource Center (SORC) is the student center designed to support all aspects of student organization operations and development.

name: Student Activity Fee Allocation Committee
summary: The Student Activity Fee Allocation Committee (SAFAC) exists to support the initiatives, goals, and activities of recognized Drexel student organizations and Student Life by allocating the student activity fees.

name: Drexel Architectural Engineering Institute
summary: Drexel AEI is a collaborative group for those interested in any aspects of the engineered world we see on a daily basis. We also oversee the Drexel's Steel Bridge Team, which competes in the annual AISC Student Steel Bridge Competition (SSBC).

name: Drexel Sierra Club
summary: Drexel Sierra Club seeks to protect and appreciate the environment by working to reduce Drexel's environmental impact and taking hikes, going camping, and other outdoor trips.

name: Pi Tau Sigma, Mechanical Engineering Honor Society
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. The Pi Tau Sigma mission is to strive to create better engineers through commitment to academic excellence and dedication to service.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers - Drexel University Student Chapter
summary: We are organization that fosters professional development, academic excellence, and leadership in the civil engineering field, bridging gaps between students and professionals by serving as a connection to City, Regional, and National sections of ASCE.

name: Drexel Women in Business
summary: Drexel Women in Business is a joint student organization. Drexel Women in Business (DWIB) is a network of dynamic, like-minded women achieving their business goals through support, inclusion, inspiration, and mentoring.

name: WKDU Philadelphia 91.7FM
summary: WKDU is an award winning, free format, non-commercial FM college radio station located in Philadelphia, PA. Broadcasting on 91.7 FM, WKDU is owned and operated by Drexel University and its students.

name: Japanese Undergraduate Student Association
summary: The Japanese Undergraduate Student Association (JUSA) welcomes everyone interested in Japanese culture. We host weekly events, cultural workshops, and group trips to D.C. and New York City to share and experience Japanese traditions together.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: SWE is an organization dedicated to promoting women in the engineering fields by providing a support network to its members, professional development opportunities, and community service opportunities. We welcome all genders and majors!

name: Campus Activities Board
summary: The Campus Activities Board (CAB) is the largest student-run event programming organization at Drexel University. Our purpose is to plan and execute a variety of events reflective of the diverse interests and needs of the undergraduate student body.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: Serving our members by enhancing their knowledge of public relations and providing access to professional development opportunities; and serving the public relations profession by helping to develop highly qualified, well-prepared professionals.

name: Drexel University Tae Kwon Do Club
summary: Drexel Tae Kwon Do Club is open to all students interested in learning martial arts and welcomes beginners to join anytime. Tae Kwon Do can help students learn balance, strength, speed, focus, and control in both the mental and physical senses.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon
summary: The only National Honor Society for the computing sciences recognized as such by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and IEEE Computer Society. Drexel University was chartered in 1989 as the fifth chapter of the state of Pennsylvania.

name: Drexel Electric Racing
summary: At Drexel Electric Racing we design, build, and race a formula-style electric racecar for the yearly Formula SAE Electric competition. Email FormulaEV@drexel.edu or svs67@drexel.edu to get started.

name: Formula SAE
summary: Drexel Formula SAE is an organization open to students of all backgrounds interested in designing, building, and racing a Formula SAE race car for an annual, international competition.

name: American Chemical Society Drexel Student Affiliates
summary: Drexel ACS provides a community based in chemistry which offers educational, creative, social, and professional resources and activities!

name: Drexel University Chapter of the Society of Physics Students
summary: Our goals are the advancement and diffusion of the academic knowledge of physics; the encouragement of interest in physics throughout the local community; the introduction of students to professional societies; fellowship, and collaboration.

name: Drexel Economics Society
summary: The Drexel Economics Society seeks to broaden the understanding of economic fundamentals amongst students of all disciplines through educational, professional, and community-building events and presence on campus. Ready to join?

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
summary: TriBeta is a biological honor society that promotes the advancement of scientific research and cultivates an appreciation for biological knowledge, with a focus on creating a community amongst its members and the faculty.

name: National Organization for Business and Engineering - Drexel Chapter
summary: The goal of the National Organization for Business and Engineering (NOBE) is to bring together students with interests in both business and engineering to corporate and academic institutions that promote the integration of these fields into one.

name: Drexel Players
summary: The Drexel Players are a student theatre organization that works to produce student written work, engage the student body through seeing shows & taking part in performances, and broaden students' knowledge & involvement in the Philadelphia theatre.

name: Phi Sigma Pi - National Honor Fraternity
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive national honor fraternity that is unique in its combination of an honor society and a social fraternity. It is a diverse campus organization that focuses on a tripod of ideals: Scholarship, Leadership, and Fellowship.

name: Unplugged Gaming Organization
summary: Social organization for board game lovers, card game enjoyers, TTRPG players, wargamers, and everyone who likes to have fun.

name: American Marketing Association Drexel
summary: The American Marketing Association Drexel aims to further students’ knowledge of all applications of marketing, while also providing the extracurricular opportunities needed to make students competitive in today’s job market.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: Want to learn more about SigEp? Fill out our interest form here: https://linktr.ee/sigep_drexel Sigma Phi Epsilon (SigEp) is a social college fraternity that was founded on three principles: Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love.

name: Mathematics Student Organization
summary: The Math Student Organization is committed to fostering a community of students with a common interest in mathematics so that they can learn about, appreciate, and have fun with all aspects of math.

name: TechServ
summary: TechServ helps bridge the Digital Divide by refurbishing and donating computers to non-profit organizations and individuals in need. We host Genius Bars where we help students and community members with computer problems. Any student can be a member!

name: TrebleMakers
summary: The TrebleMakers are the only women-centered a cappella group at Drexel. We host concerts on campus, perform at events across the country, and compete in the ICCAs competition.

name: Chemical and Biological Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: Providing academic, social, and professional development events/opportunities for Chemical Engineering graduate students and others graduate associations.

name: Biology & BEES Graduate Student Association
summary: The BGSA is an organization dedicated to building a strong social and academic community through the unification of graduate students from the Department of Biology and the Department of Biodiversity, Earth, and Environmental Science.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
summary: The American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics student chapter at Drexel University is a student organization that is dedicated to the continuing education of its members in the field of aerospace science and engineering.

name: Drexel Veterans Association
summary: The Drexel Veterans Association was founded to connect veterans with one another, both in school and in the community.

name: Delta Phi Epsilon
summary: Delta Phi Epsilon's motto is "Esse Quam Videri" which means "be rather than to seem to be"; Our values are Justice, Sisterhood, and Love.

name: Drexel Graduate Women in Science and Engineering
summary: Drexel Graduate Women in Science and Engineering is focused on expanding and enhancing the experiences of all the women and non-binary graduates in Science and Engineering by organizing events that will contribute to their professional development.

name: Drexel Black Graduate Student Union
summary: The mission of DBGSU is to foster an environment of intellectual, political, cultural, and social growth for those who want to connect with Black graduate and professional degree-seeking students.

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
summary: We promote communication within the physics community and our peers within the Drexel graduate community.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association at Drexel University
summary: CSSAD established in March 2009, aiming to provide support to all Chinese students and scholars who are adjusting to the new environment as well as to the college life. CSSAD launches core values of selfless contribution, responsibility, and innovation.

name: Drexel University Construction Association
summary: The DUCA is dedicated to promoting and facilitating the professional development of Drexel University students concentrating in studies including Construction Management, Real Estate, Engineering, and Architecture.

name: Psi Chi Honor Society
summary: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship and to advance the science of psychology. Membership is open to students who meet minimum qualifications.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: The American Institute of Chemical Engineers is a globally recognized organization for the chemical engineering profession. For over 100 years AIChE has been working to distinguish and define chemical engineering as a profession.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: Our mission: “To increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community”

name: Global China Connection
summary: Global China Connection (GCC) is a nonprofit and non-partisan organization that centers activations around cultural exchange for Drexel students interested in Chinese culture and the like. All students are welcome!

name: Drexel SIGGRAPH
summary: Organization focused on Digital Media and computer graphics. We have student-run lectures on special topics within Digital Media, screenings of animated and effects-heavy movies, and are committed to a strong presence at the annual SIGGRAPH conference.

name: Wilbur W. Oaks Physician Assistant Student Society
summary: This society serves as the official organization of the Drexel University Physician Assistant Program.

name: Theme Park Engineering and Design Group
summary: The Drexel Theme Park Engineering and Design Group (Drexel TPED) is an undergraduate organization at Drexel University that celebrates the themed entertainment industry! For more information, please visit our website: www.drexeltped.org

name: Delta Sigma Phi Fraternity
summary: "Better Men, Better Lives"

name: International Graduate Student Association
summary: The mission of this organization shall be to advocate the interests, address the concerns, improve the communication channels, and augment the life of international graduate students at Drexel University.

name: Future Educators of Drexel
summary: Future Educators of Drexel (FEDs) strives to further the involvement of its members in the field of education through community and social opportunities, as well as affiliation with relevant professional organizations and educational institutions.

name: Drexel Criminal Justice Honors Society
summary: The honor society is primarily geared towards assisting upperclassmen in obtaining employment post graduation through creating relationships with individuals and agencies in the criminal justice field.

name: Lindy Center for Civic Engagement
summary:
Mission statement:
Drexel University’s Lindy Center for Civic Engagement promotes the ideals of social responsibility and public service by facilitating community based experiential learning for students, faculty, and staff. Through collaborations with t

name: The Good Idea Fund
summary: The Good Idea Fund exists as a way for individual students, faculty, staff, and recognized student organizations to apply and access student activity fee funds to engage the undergraduate student body.

name: The Cleftomaniacs
summary: The Cleftomaniacs, Drexel's first all-gender a cappella group established in 2012!

name: Drexel Gaming Association
summary: We are Drexel University's home for gaming. In addition to hosting quarterly LANs, we have frequent community events - everything from board games to tournaments!

name: Communication, Culture, and Media Association of Doctoral Students (CCMADS)
summary: CCMADS is a student organization for current Ph.D. students in the Department of Culture and Communication. The club will meets monthly to discuss upcoming events, member activity and departmental needs.

name: Drexel Student Centers
summary: Drexel Student Centers manages the daily operations of the Creese Student Center and Ross Commons, creating and managing work order flow and building projects for 135,000 square feet of space.

name: Materials Research Society
summary: The Materials Research Society (MRS) Drexel Chapter promotes interdisciplinary materials research on contemporary topics. Seminars, workshops, and discussions are held to understand and update our knowledge of cutting edge Material Science research.

name: International Students and Scholars Services
summary: International Students and Scholar Services

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers: Drexel University Chapter
summary: The Society of Asian Scientists & Engineers (SASE) aims to help scientists, engineers, and technologists reach their full career potential through professional development, cultural awareness, and giving back to their community.

name: The Neuroscience Society
summary: NSS strives to facilitate university-wide discussions of neuroscience through hosting discussions and events on a multitude of topics in the field of neuroscience that may interest our members and expand our knowledge.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers - Drexel Chapter
summary: SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance & achieve the potential of Hispanics in Science, Technology, Engineering,& Math. We plan to coordinate events that would introduce students to the professional world.

name: Supply Chain Management Association
summary: SCMA empowers Drexel's future Supply Chain leaders by enriching the knowledge and career development of our members. SCMA encourages interest and promotes the importance of supply chain management education and networking through LeBow C.o.B.

name: College of Computing and Informatics Doctoral Student Association
summary: Organization dedicated to developing and maintaining a social and academic community among CCI PhD students.

name: Van Rensselaer Hall
summary: Anything and everything Van R related. Catch up on news, social media, and events taking place within Van R.

name: Drexel CyberDragons
summary: This organization is for students interested in cybersecurity that want to learn more about the field and/or compete in collegiate competitions.

name: The Accounting Society
summary: The Accounting Society (TAS) of Drexel University aims to prepare all accounting students to become leaders through the betterment of professional demeanor and foundational understandings.

name: Dragon Concert Series
summary: A student organization dedicated to music industry career development and hands on experience in live music!

name: Drexel Criminal Justice Society
summary: We will have a meeting once a week on Drexel campus to schedule, discuss, and participate in events that are in the criminal justice field. We will also have field trips and community events in Philadelphia.

name: Drexel University Real Estate Club
summary: Our club brings together and connects Drexel Students with the most prestigious professionals in Philadelphia Real Estate. We provide Drexel Students with educational sessions and networking events that enrich their personal and professional knowledge.

name: Neurodragons
summary: Neurodragons is a dedicated body of neurodivergent and neurotypical students committed to raising awareness of the value of neurodiversity within the Drexel learning community and beyond.

name: South Asian Students Association
summary: Drexel University's South Asian Student Association (SASA) looks to foster an all-inclusive community and aims to increase the awareness of South Asian students by participating in and hosting social, professional, service, and cultural-driven events.

name: Retail and Design Club
summary: Retail and Design Club is an organization that offers students the opportunity to immerse in the fashion industry through on campus events, speakers, and networking. Open to all Drexel Students.

name: Dragon's First
summary: A student organization that supports first-generation college students and students from a low-Income background at Drexel University through mentorship, networking, workshops, and social events.

name: North Hall
summary: North Hall

name: Towers Hall
summary: Towers Hall

name: Kelly Hall
summary: Kelly Hall

name: Kappa Delta Pi
summary: National Society of Education Chartered at Drexel June 7th, 2017 Purpose: To foster excellence in education and promote fellowship among those dedicated to teaching.

name: International Council on Systems Engineering
summary: The purpose Drexel INCOSE is to provide an enriched environment for systems engineering students to learn and engage with professionals in the field of systems engineering outside the classroom environment.

name: Drexel Motion Capture
summary: To further the knowledge of motion capture technology through self guided and group oriented projects. We will engage our peers and provide an opportunity for discussions on special topics that utilize Drexel’s many resources in our fields of study.

name: LeBow Student Leadership
summary: LeBow's Dean's Student Advisory Board and Peer Leaders will be working together to create a great impact on the LeBow College of Business.

name: Drexel Society of Artificial Intelligence
summary: Drexel AI promotes the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML), increases awareness, and helps students get familiar with the state-of-art technological applications of AI

name: Drexel CHI User Experience Club
summary: Drexel CHI User Experience Club is a campus chapter of ACM SIGCHI. We are dedicated to providing students with interest in user experience, computer-human interaction, and design a place to share ideas, collaborate and develop professionally.

name: School of Education Graduate Student Organization
summary: This organization aims to provide professional development and networking opportunities to the graduate students in the School of Education. The School of Education Graduate Student Association is for all Masters, Ed.D, and Ph.D students.

name: Sanitation Health Aid Relief Project
summary: Sanitation Health Aid Relief Project (SHARP) is a student-run organization that aims to address water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) challenges both locally in Philadelphia and on an international scale.

name: Club Golf
summary: The focus of the Drexel Golf team is to promote the game of golf to those who would like to begin or continue to play the game at a competitive level. The team fills the gap between playing for Drexel's division one golf team and playing for fun.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is the nation’s leading co-ed professional business fraternity, with a commitment to equipping our members throughout their careers to make a greater impact in their business, civic, and personal lives.

name: Lambda Alpha Upsilon
summary: Latino America Unida, Lambda Alpha Upsilon Fraternity, Inc.

name: Special Events and Free In Philadelphia
summary: EMSS Special Events and Free in Philadelphia - A listing of free events and programs for students!

name: Drexel Dhvani
summary: Dhvani is Drexel's only South Asian A Capella group! We perform original arrangements, and are excited to join the collegiate network for A Capella. We strive to share our passion for music and culture with others looking to explore this innovative style

name: Pennoni Honors College
summary: Pennoni Honors College

name: Center for Black Culture
summary: The Center for Black Culture (CBC) is a university department that serves as a hub of information, activity, and community for Black students, faculty, staff, and alumni.

name: Saving Mothers
summary: Saving Mothers is an organization dedicated to raising awareness and funds for underprivileged mothers and pregnant women in need of reproductive health care services.

name: I AM ME
summary: I am ME is a professional organization at Drexel focused on promoting equal partnership, equal opportunities, and increased visibility for female engineers in Mechanical Engineering. We will educate members through workshops, seminars, and more.

name: Hack4Impact Drexel
summary: Building powerful nonprofit software as a tool for social good. We help nonprofits do the good they are meant to be doing more efficiently. By building them the custom software they need, they can focus on their important work.

name: Drexel Business Services
summary: To be updated

name: Perfect Pair - Drexel University Chapter
summary: Individualized one-on-one through an extensive matching process that considers background, hobbies, and interests, college volunteers are paired with a senior in a local assisted living facility.

name: Writers Room
summary: Writers Room is a university-community literary arts program engaged in creative placemaking and art for social justice. Our mission is to develop inclusive, intergenerational, co-creative places that foster connection and community.

name: Climate and Sustainability EcoReps
summary:
Climate and Sustainability’s EcoReps Program is a group of student environmental leaders dedicated to increasing both opportunities for student awareness of sustainability on campus.
https://bit.ly/EcoRepsLinkTree
Insta:sustainabledrexel
Wednesdays 2-4

name: Lit Lab
summary: Lit Lab was created in an effort to bridge the gap between STEM and the humanities together through a student-run blog that publishes submissions quarterly

name: Drexel International Business Student Association
summary: Our organization aims to create a community of culturally diverse members by arranging various speaker events and focusing on career-oriented programming. Members will increase their global awareness and grow professionally.

name: Drexel Business of Fashion
summary: Drexel Business of Fashion is a community of like-minded people where business meets fashion. Our organization provides students with opportunities to learn about the business side of fashion, attend thematic events, and network.

name: DUCOM Journal Club
summary: Journal Club

name: Hematology/Oncology Interest Group
summary: The aim of the DUCOM Hematology/Oncology interest group is to help educate students on Heme/Onc and expose them to different elements of the field so that they can make informed professional and career decisions. Join our GroupMe with the link below!

name: LGBTQIA+ Professionals in Medicine
summary: LGBTQIA+ Professionals in Medicine seeks to enhance the health of the LGBTQIA+ community

name: East Asian Students and Scholars Association
summary: EASSA is a multicultural group which seeks to provide a social network to the scholars and scientists at DUCOM and to help them develop their careers and promote their academic success.

name: Ophthalmology Interest Group
summary: Ophthalmology Interest Group

name: DUCOM Stories in Medicine
summary: DUCOM Stories in Medicine

name: Primary Care Progress at Drexel
summary: Primary Care Progress at Drexel

name: Dermatology Interest Group
summary: Dermatology Interest Group

name: Multimedia Audio for Westphal
summary: Our mission is to facilitate community and collaborations between Westphal students with interests or projects relating to audio for games and extended media.

name: Groove Phi Groove
summary: Groove Phi Groove

name: Middle Eastern/North African in Medicine
summary:
As an organization, we hope to fundraise and bring awareness to the MENA region.
This will include providing mentorship for undergraduate interested in medicine. In addition, we will encourage a sense of community among students who identify with MENA.

name: DUCOM Reading Terminal Society
summary: Learning society dedicated to improving student lives through events, accommodating communication between students and staff, and overall efforts in improving student wellbeing

name: Office of Community Health and Inclusive Excellence
summary: The Office of Community Health & Inclusive Excellence at Drexel University College of Medicine provides a platform for inter-professional, multidisciplinary community engagement and health equity activities and collaboration across Drexel University.

name: Academic Medicine Interest Group
summary: The Academic Medicine Interest Group (AMIG) aims to bring awareness to DUCOM students about what a career in academic medicine entails. This includes what academic medicine is, the different careers in academia, and the path to academic medicine.

name: Film in Color
summary: We provide a space where film students of underrepresented identities can get to know one another to collaborate on projects in and out of the club, along with providing networking opportunities with those currently in the industry.

name: National Council of Negro Women, Inc. Drexel Section
summary: The National Council of Negro Women is an “organization of organizations” comprised of 330 campus and community-based sections and 33 national women’s organizations that enlightens, inspires, and connects more than 2,000,000 women and men.

name: Radial Groove
summary: A group designed to bring in musicians of all genres to discuss and appreciate music regularly, host private and public jam sessions, and inform the DUCOM community about local concerts.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is the world’s preeminent Pre-law fraternity because of our dedication to service: service to the student, the school, the profession, and the community.

name: American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists - Drexel University Chapter
summary: The American Academy of Environmental Engineers and Scientists (AAEES) is a professional organization that aims to support and connect undergraduate and graduate environmental engineering and science students as they navigate their degrees and careers.

name: DUCOM Yarn Dragons
summary:
Yarn Dragons is an organization designed to spread our love of crafts and to make a positive impact on the community. 

Our focus is primarily on the Philadelphia campus.

name: Women in Orthopaedics
summary: Women in Orthopaedics is an orthopaedic society which promotes mentorship and empowerment of all those who identify as female and are interested in/pursuing a career in the field of orthopaedics.

name: Allergy and Immunology Interest Group
summary: The Allergy and Immunology Interest Group aims to educate students about the Allergy and Immunology specialty of internal medicine.

name: Toxic Free Philly Drexel
summary: TFPD focuses on mitigating environmental toxin exposure on campus and in the greater Philadelphia community, having started with synthetic herbicides and lead poisoning. Our work includes advocacy and research surrounding environmental toxins.

name: Drexel DECA
summary: DECA's mission is to prepare emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality, and management. DECA will engage students personally, professionally, and academically through workshops, competitions, and mentorship opportunities.

name: Bentley Hall
summary: Welcome to Bentley Hall!

name: Race Hall
summary: Welcome to Race Hall

name: Master of Science in Clinical Optometry
summary: TEMP

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearing Association (NSSLHA) is the only national student organization for pre-professionals studying communication sciences and disorders (CSD) recognized by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).

name: PCO Contact Lens Club
summary: Program specific organization that hosts events and workshops for members of the program interested in furthering their contact lens education

name: Private Practice Club
summary: The purpose of the Private Practice Club is to prepare optometry students at Pennsylvania College of Optometry to enter private/ independent optometry practice by providing a knowledge base about the business aspects of the profession.

name: Central Asian Student Organization
summary: Central Asian Student Organization (CASO) celebrates Central Asian cultures through cultural events, educational programs, and community building, creating an inclusive space for students at Drexel University.

name: Drexel Exercise Science Club
summary: This organization aims to support students in exercise science by creating an inclusive, educational community that offers resources, opportunities, and mentorship while reducing academic, financial, and experiential barriers.

name: Political Science Student Association
summary: The Political Science Student Association is a student led organization that fosters community among political science students, encourages engagement with the field’s diverse disciplines, and promotes the practical application of political science.

name: ColorStack Chapter At Drexel University
summary: The purpose(s) of this organization shall be to increase the number of Black and Latinx Computer Science graduates that go on to launch rewarding technical careers.

name: Drexel Black Pre-Law Association
summary: Drexel Black Pre-Law Association (BPLA)’s mission statement is to promote diversity within the legal field by providing mentorship, resources, and opportunities that prepare our members for law school and eventually, their career.
